title: Past-only Kalman tire-state residuals
summary: A 2026 F1 tire-degradation paper shows state-space models outperform simple AR baselines and treat pit stops as reset events.
rationale: A 2026 F1 tire-degradation paper shows state-space models outperform simple AR baselines and treat pit stops as reset events. The current baseline uses static degradation heuristics but not an online latent state. A fold-safe version can infer driver-race-stint latent wear, pace drift, and uncertainty from past laps only, then feed those states into the tabular model.
implementation_hint: Within each Race-Year-Driver sequence, fit a lightweight local-level/Kalman filter on lap-time residuals after removing race/lap medians; reset at PitStop=1; emit filtered state, state slope, innovation, and state variance at each lap. Refit/estimate using train folds only, then generate features forward-only for validation/test.
expected_effect: Better separation of true tire collapse vs noisy slow laps, especially late in stints and on atypical compounds; likely small CV gain with better leaderboard robustness.
risk: Easy to leak if smoothed states or full-race normalization are used. Overly complex filtering may add noise if lap-time residualization is weak.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
